metadata.name: Military Retirement System Rules
metadata.description: Comprehensive rules for all four military retirement systems: Final Pay, High-3, REDUX/CSB, and Blended Retirement System (BRS). Covers eligibility, calculation formulas, COLA rules, and survivor benefit plan.
notes: Military retirement system is determined by date of initial entry into military service (DIEMS).
All systems require 20+ years of active duty service for immediate retirement (or 20 qualifying years for reserve retirement).
Disability retirement available with fewer than 20 years under different rules.
Military retired pay is taxable as ordinary income at the federal level.

retirement_systems.status: Legacy — very few active members remain
retirement_systems.cola_rule: Full CPI-W (same as Social Security COLA)

retirement_systems.applies_to: DIEMS on or after September 8, 1980 and before January 1, 2018 (who did not opt into BRS)
retirement_systems.pay_base: Average of highest 36 months of basic pay
retirement_systems.cola_rule: Full CPI-W (same as Social Security COLA)

example.description: 20 years × 2.5% = 50% of high-3 average basic pay

retirement_systems.applies_to: DIEMS on or after August 1, 1986 and before January 1, 2018. Must have elected CSB at 15 years of service.
retirement_systems.status: Legacy — option eliminated for those entering after 2018
retirement_systems.formula: high_3_average × (2.5% - 1% reduction for each year under 30) × years_of_service
retirement_systems.multiplier_note: Effective 2.0% per year at 20 years due to REDUX formula, but catches up to 2.5% at 30 years
retirement_systems.pay_base: Average of highest 36 months of basic pay
retirement_systems.cola_rule: CPI-W minus 1% (partial COLA — purchasing power erodes over time)
retirement_systems.cola_catchup: One-time recomputation to full CPI at age 62, then COLA-1% resumes
retirement_systems.csb_note: $30,000 Career Status Bonus paid at 15 years in exchange for accepting REDUX formula

example.description: 20 years × 2.0% effective = 40% of high-3 (vs 50% under High-3 system)

retirement_systems.applies_to: DIEMS on or after January 1, 2018. Optional opt-in was available in 2018 for those with fewer than 12 years of service.
retirement_systems.pay_base: Average of highest 36 months of basic pay
retirement_systems.cola_rule: Full CPI-W (same as Social Security COLA)

tsp_match_details.automatic_contribution_note: DoD contributes 1% of basic pay regardless of member contribution
tsp_match_details.vesting: 2 years of service for automatic 1%; matching vests immediately
tsp_match_details.notes: Maximum DoD contribution is 5% of basic pay (1% auto + 4% match on 5% member contribution)

continuation_pay_details.notes: Amount varies by service branch and occupational specialty

lump_sum_option.notes: Member can elect to receive 25% or 50% of retired pay as lump sum at retirement, with reduced monthly annuity until age 67 (SS FRA), then full amount resumes

example.description: 20 years × 2.0% = 40% of high-3 average (lower than High-3 system, but offset by TSP matching)

reserve_retirement.eligibility: 20 qualifying years of service (must have 50+ retirement points per year)
reserve_retirement.early_pay_provision: Age reduced by 90 days for each 90 days of active duty in a fiscal year after January 28, 2008 (earliest age 50)
reserve_retirement.notes: Reserve/Guard retirement pay begins at age 60 (or earlier per early pay provision). Points-based system.

survivor_benefit_plan.description: SBP provides up to 55% of retired pay to eligible survivors
survivor_benefit_plan.base_amount_options: Full retired pay or any lesser amount (minimum $300/month)
survivor_benefit_plan.social_security_offset: Eliminated by NDAA FY2023 — SBP-DIC offset fully repealed as of 2023
survivor_benefit_plan.paid_up_provision: Premiums stop after 360 months of payments AND reaching age 70

crdp.name: Concurrent Retirement and Disability Pay
crdp.eligibility: 20+ year retirees with 50%+ VA disability rating
crdp.effect: Restores full military retired pay previously offset by VA disability compensation

crsc.name: Combat-Related Special Compensation
crsc.effect: Tax-free payment equal to VA disability compensation amount for combat-related conditions
crsc.notes: Cannot receive both CRDP and CRSC — must elect one
